Contact Us School Updates

The Founders & Leadership

Precious English Medium School was built on a simple dream: that every child in Qacha's Nek deserves the very best start in life.

Co-Founder & Director

Mr. Raymond

A passionate educator and visionary leader, Mr. Raymond has dedicated his life to ensuring that children across Qacha's Nek have access to quality English medium education. His belief that language opens doors drives everything at Precious School.

Leadership Curriculum
Co-Founder & Head Teacher

Mrs. Norah

Mrs. Norah is the heart of Precious School. With warmth, tireless dedication, and an unshakeable belief in every child's potential, she nurtures learners from their very first day and champions cultural pride alongside academic excellence.

Early Childhood Culture & Values

Community Heart

Our Academic Blocks

From the very first year of learning to the final primary grade, every Precious School child is surrounded by care, curiosity, and joy.

Ages 2 – 6

Pre-School Program

Baby Class · Pre-K · Kindergarten. The foundation years built on play, song, storytelling, and love.

Baby Class Pre-K Kindergarten
Grades 1 – 7

Primary School Program

A rigorous, joyful English-medium curriculum from Grade 1 through Grade 7. Building thinkers, readers, and future leaders of Lesotho.

English Medium STEM Grades 1–7
Special Event

Career Day

Our little learners dress up as their dream professions — doctors, soldiers, engineers. Big dreams start at Precious School.

Inspiring Young Minds

Dream Big, Always

At Precious School, we inspire children to look beyond the horizon and imagine every future as possible for them.

Community Notice Board

Stay informed — important messages for parents, guardians, and the Precious School family.

Term 3 · 2026

Term 3 Opening Date – 14 July 2026

All learners return on Tuesday, 14 July 2026. Please ensure school fees are settled by the end of the first week. Uniforms must be worn on the first day back.

Parents' Meeting · July 2026

Mid-Year Parent–Teacher Consultations

Report cards will be issued and individual consultations scheduled for the week of 21 July 2026. All parents are strongly encouraged to attend.

School Fee Notice

Fees & Payment Arrangements

Parents who require a payment plan for Term 3 fees are asked to visit the school office before 30 June 2026 to make arrangements with Mrs. Norah.

Cultural Event · August 2026

Annual Culture Day Celebration

Our beloved Culture Day returns in August. Learners are encouraged to come in traditional Basotho dress. All are welcome!

Academic · Term 3

Grade 7 Examination Preparation

Grade 7 learners begin exam preparation in Term 3. Extra-study sessions every Wednesday and Friday from 3 PM.

Health & Safety

Winter Wellness Reminder

Please ensure your child is dressed warmly including the full school tracksuit. Children who appear unwell must stay home and rest.
